title: Crucible Health: inference latency spike + transient 5xx on /v1/generate (INC-9821)
preview:
Customer escalation relayed by Stephanie Nguyen. Crucible Health reported sudden latency spike and burst of 5xx on inference endpoint /v1/generate beginning ~2026-08-28 07:55 PDT. Reported impact: median latency increased from ~120ms to ~900ms; throughput dropped from ~250 req/s to ~150 req/s for Crucible production key; initial customer-observed 5xx rate ~8% during the window; multiple batches timed out and downstream throttles triggered. Customer had a critical demo at 10:00 PDT.

Immediate hypotheses (serving): autoscaler oscillation under burst + degraded single shard causing head-of-line blocking.

Immediate mitigations executed: emergency scale-up of dedicated pool (target +20% capacity) + temporary concurrency limit increase + drain suspected bad instance(s) + enable routing of new requests to compatible fallback model variant (lower context) to reduce per-token work.

Outcome (updated after investigation): improvements observed within ~9–12 minutes; throughput returned to baseline and tail latency normalized after draining the degraded node(s) and reverting autoscaler policy.

Updated root cause (post-incident): the triggering event was an autoscaler policy update that reduced stabilization/cooldown and interacted poorly with a burst in queue depth, causing scale oscillation. One GPU instance concurrently entered a degraded driver state (intermittent kernel launch stalls rather than a hard OOM), amplifying tail latency and increasing request timeouts which surfaced as 5xx at the gateway. The instance replacement lag plus oscillation extended the incident duration.

Impact (finalized): peak 5xx rate revised to ~6–8% (varied by minute) with ~10–12 minute period of elevated errors; throughput dipped to ~60% of baseline at trough. No evidence of data integrity or security issues.

title: Containment & fast-snapshot restore for tenant optimizer surge causing tail latency spikes
preview:
Issue summary:
BrightCart reported a sudden increase in 95th/99th percentile token latency beginning 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC after an automated optimizer profile rollout. The change coincided with enabling aggressive batching + prefix-prefetch + an int8 quantization profile for several tenant routes.

Impact:
- Multiple customer application endpoints experienced 95/99p token-latency spikes from ~120ms to 900-1200ms.
- Elevated request queueing and ~2% 5xx spike observed for BrightCart tenant routes during the window.
- Affects revenue-critical checkout flow (routes pinned to dedicated pool).

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity pool: dc-dedicated-09 (us-east-1)
- Tenant: brightcart-prod (dedicated)
- Models: rw-3.0-q4_0 (primary), rw-7b-int8 (fallback)
- Runtime version: 2026.02.28-release
- Optimizer profile applied automatically via policy: opt-profile-2026-03-12-agg-v2

Steps to reproduce / observed sequence:
1) At 09:08 UTC, Canary job rolled opt-profile-2026-03-12-agg-v2 to 10% traffic across pool.
2) At 09:10 UTC, automatic progressive rollout reached 75% due to permissive success checks.
3) At 09:11–09:40 UTC, BrightCart reported rising tail latencies and increased retries.
4) At 09:25 UTC, automated scaling attempted to add GPU nodes but queuing persisted.

Logs & metrics (excerpt):
- Serving runtime logs: "prefetch_queue_depth spike=187 host=gc-09"
- Kernel scheduler: "batch_assembly_time p99=780ms"
- Request traces: "kv_cache_miss_ratio increased from 4%->44% after rollout"
- Error rate: 5xx increased from 0.3%->2.4% for tenant brightcart-prod

Initial investigation notes:
- Hypothesis A: aggressive prefix-prefetch caused excessive KV eviction thrashing at the same time quantized kernels increased compute op variance, amplifying tail latency under mixed sequence lengths.
- Hypothesis B: the progressive rollout applied a mixed-optimizer setting without a tenant-scoped snapshot, so tenant-specific cache/eviction parameters changed globally for the pool leading to noisy-neighbor effects despite dedicated capacity labeling.
- Evidence: tenant-specific traces show repeated cache repopulation and longer batch assembly windows only for tenant routes. Host-level metrics show higher memory churn concurrent with rollout.

Immediate containment performed by support (actions taken):
- 09:45 UTC: Rolled back opt-profile at control-plane level for the brightcart-prod tenant using quick-snapshot restore of opt-profile-2026-02-27-stable. This was executed via the per-tenant snapshot restore API to avoid pool-wide changes.
- 09:48 UTC: Applied temporary rate-cap for aggressive routes and enabled conservative batching policy (max_batch_tokens reduced).
- 10:05 UTC: Observed 95p latency fall back to ~160–220ms within 15 minutes, 99p trending down to ~400ms. 5xx returned to baseline by 10:20 UTC.

Proposed medium-term mitigations:
- Add preflight simulation for aggressive optimizer profiles that runs tenant-seeded KV/cache stress tests and batch-assembly latency projection before progressive rollout.
- Enforce per-tenant gating for optimizer parameters that impact cache layout and eviction heuristics (prefix-prefetch, eviction_threshold).
- Add rollout slow-start guard: abort progressive increases if per-tenant kv_cache_miss_ratio increases > 10% over baseline in the first 5 minutes.

Action items assigned:
- Support: provide BrightCart with timeline and mitigation summary (assigned to Priya).
- SRE/Runtime: instrument per-tenant eviction tracing and add an alert for kv_cache_miss_ratio (Marcus). Linked PR created: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/1824
- Product: update optimizer rollout policy to require tenant preflight for dedicated pools (product ticket created).

Next steps / outstanding questions:
- Confirm whether BrightCart used any tenant overrides that could have forced a non-snapshoted optimizer flag change. BrightCart to provide their console change history.
- Validate snapshot integrity: ensure snapshot restoration is atomic and includes eviction config and quantization flags.

Resolution notes (pending):
- Containment succeeded with per-tenant quick-snapshot restore. We will keep the ticket open until per-tenant preflight gating is implemented and a post-incident runbook is published.

title: Per-tenant delivery receipts, watermarking, and compliance evidence crosswalk request
preview:
Issue summary: Customer BrightHealth Analytics requests a bundled compliance artifact that demonstrates per-tenant ordered delivery, per-event delivery receipts, persistent watermarking (monotonic event sequence), and a crosswalk mapping between the audit fields we provide and the customer SIEM schema.

Impact: Customer needs this artifact to complete a data residency + SIEM compliance package for their SOC review. Without the bundle they cannot finalize onboarding of a regulated workload, delaying an enterprise contract.

Environment: Production (us-east-1). Customer is on enterprise plan with private ingestion endpoints and uses our tenant-scoped downstream webhooks into their Splunk HEC cluster.

Request details: BrightHealth expects: (1) a per-tenant manifest showing monotonic watermarks and last-applied offsets for the period 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28, (2) delivery-receipt logs for webhook POSTs including HTTP status, retries, and at-least-once/duplicate indicators, (3) a CSV crosswalk mapping Redwood audit fields to BrightHealth SIEM fields along with example events, and (4) cryptographic checksum (SHA256) for each daily file for chain-of-custody.

Preferred format: gzipped NDJSON for events, CSV for crosswalk, and a JSON manifest that lists file checksums and per-file watermark ranges. Customer also requested guidance on how our replay endpoints guarantee ordering during backfill.
text:

title: Sudden latency and tail-time spike in dedicated pool following tenant preemption/eviction events
preview: Issue summary: Since ~2026-03-10T14:30Z, NimbleAI has reported a sharp increase in median and p99 latency for requests routed to their dedicated GPU pool. The spike correlates with a series of preemptive eviction events and rapid tenant slot reassignments triggered by autoscaler activity. Impact: Customer-facing API calls experienced 5–12x higher p99 tail latency for ~40 minutes; several streaming sessions disconnected mid-stream. This is impacting production throughput and user-facing SLAs. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated-az-usw2-03), prod traffic, region us-west-2. Steps observed/repro: 1) High short-lived burst from tenant A caused scheduler to preempt idle slots and reassign GPUs. 2) Eviction sequence triggered slot draining and KV-cache invalidations. 3) Tenants B and C received sudden allocation changes causing KV cache misses and rewarm overhead. 4) Requests experienced elevated token generation times and streaming disconnects. Observed behaviour: - p50 increased from ~80ms to ~450ms, p99 from ~320ms to ~3.2s. - Autoscaler logs show a flurry of preemption operations and immediate re-alloc attempts. - GPU utilization shows hotspots on a subset of nodes while others sit underutilized. - Eviction events include preemption_reason: "policy-burst" in scheduler logs. Expected behaviour: Autoscaler/scheduler should smooth transitions, avoid mass simultaneous preemptions, and maintain KV cache warm paths to prevent full rewarm for colocated tenants. Initial mitigation applied: 1) Support temporarily raised eviction cooldown for NimbleAI's pool to reduce churn. 2) Forced routing of high-latency requests to a compatible fallback model version (lower-memory) for the worst-affecting routes. 3) Notified on-call infra team and placed watch on autoscaler tunables.
text: Issue summary: Since ~2026-03-10T14:30Z, NimbleAI has reported a sharp increase in median and p99 latency for requests routed to their dedicated GPU pool. The spike correlates with a series of preemptive eviction events and rapid tenant slot reassignments triggered by autoscaler activity. Impact: Customer-facing API calls experienced 5–12x higher p99 tail latency for ~40 minutes; several streaming sessions disconnected mid-stream. This is impacting production throughput and user-facing SLAs. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated-az-usw2-03), prod traffic, region us-west-2. Steps observed/repro: 1) High short-lived burst from tenant A caused scheduler to preempt idle slots and reassign GPUs. 2) Eviction sequence triggered slot draining and KV-cache invalidations. 3) Tenants B and C received sudden allocation changes causing KV cache misses and rewarm overhead. 4) Requests experienced elevated token generation times and streaming disconnects. Observed behaviour: - p50 increased from ~80ms to ~450ms, p99 from ~320ms to ~3.2s. - Autoscaler logs show a flurry of preemption operations and immediate re-alloc attempts. - GPU utilization shows hotspots on a subset of nodes while others sit underutilized. - Eviction events include preemption_reason: "policy-burst" in scheduler logs. Expected behaviour: Autoscaler/scheduler should smooth transitions, avoid mass simultaneous preemptions, and maintain KV cache warm paths to prevent full rewarm for colocated tenants. Initial mitigation applied: 1) Support temporarily raised eviction cooldown for NimbleAI's pool to reduce churn. 2) Forced routing of high-latency requests to a compatible fallback model version (lower-memory) for the worst-affecting routes. 3) Notified on-call infra team and placed watch on autoscaler tunables.

title: Request: add/assign 'Quality Gate Override Approver' role for Optimize time-bound overrides (Console RBAC)
preview:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in Console without using a shared admin account.

What is needed:
1) Create (or confirm) a dedicated RBAC role/group for this capability:
   - Role name (proposal): quality_gate_override_approver
   - Group name (proposal): optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers
2) Permissions required (least privilege):
   - Approve/deny time-bound override requests for Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant profile)
   - View gate run details + artifacts links sufficient to make decision
   - View audit log entries related to overrides (read-only)
   - No ability to edit gating thresholds, disable gating globally, or directly apply configs outside the approved override workflow
3) Scope:
   - Hosted API (prod) routes/accounts participating in limited rollout
   - Console UI + underlying approval endpoint

Requested users to be added (initial set):
- Paula Mendes (PM, Rollouts/telemetry) — backup approver for business-hours incidents
- Sean Gallagher (SRE lead) — oncall escalation approver for after-hours/high-severity customer impact

Constraints / policy alignment:
- Overrides must be time-bound and require reason + ticket link per policy
- Approver actions must be captured in audit logs (who/when/what/expiration)
- Prefer SSO group mapping if available; otherwise Console-native group assignment is acceptable for beta

Why now:
We had a near-miss where an optimization change needed rapid override to unblock a customer, but the only available path was a full admin role (too broad). We want an audited, limited approver role before expanding rollout.

Acceptance criteria:
- Named users can approve/deny override requests in Console
- Actions write audit events visible in audit log export
- Users cannot perform broader admin actions (validated via negative test)
- Security sign-off on scope/permissions

Notes:
If feasible, also include ability for Release Engineering oncall to temporarily be added during incident response with a 24h expiration on membership.

title: Break-glass access request audit — time-bound elevation for Private control plane role binding fix (Northbridge POC)
preview:
Request type: Break-glass (emergency admin elevation)

Requester: Colin O'Donnell (Solutions Eng, Private customer pilot lead)
Requested by (ticket reporter): Fiona McCarthy (SecOps)
System(s): Redwood Private Control Plane (customer VPC), RBAC v2 enabled (pilot)
Customer context: Northbridge Healthcare (Private POC)

Reason / business justification:
- During a scheduled POC validation window, Northbridge admins were blocked from exporting audit logs and rotating an API key due to a mis-scoped role binding created during initial bootstrap.
- Customer impact: POC validation blocked; risk of missing security sign-off milestone.
- Standard path (customer admin fixes via Console) not possible because the affected binding controlled Console admin access.

Scope of requested access:
- Minimal actions required:
  1) Read RBAC bindings for org "northbridge-healthcare" in the private control plane
  2) Add a single temporary role binding granting "Read-only Auditor" and "Security Admin" to the customer-admin IdP group (SAML group mapping)
  3) Verify audit log export endpoint returns 200 and that authorization decisions are being logged
- Explicitly out of scope:
  - No model data access
  - No prompt/content inspection
  - No changes to routing policies, deployment config, or retention settings

Requested duration:
- 90 minutes time-bound elevation
- Elevation window requested: 2025-02-11 18:30–20:00 PT

Approvals (per break-glass policy):
- Approver #1 (Security Eng): Marcus Lin — APPROVED 2025-02-11 18:22 PT
- Approver #2 (SecOps/on-call): Fiona McCarthy — APPROVED 2025-02-11 18:24 PT
- FYI/exec visibility (not required but notified due to regulated customer): Dr. Aisha Rahman — ACK 2025-02-11 18:26 PT

Access method:
- Break-glass role: "redwood.breakglass.admin" (time-bound)
- Mechanism: Just-in-time elevation via internal admin service (BGW), scoped to org_id=org_7Qp1N (Northbridge Healthcare)
- MFA: required and verified

Execution notes (what was done):
- Verified current bindings: customer-admin IdP group mapped to an outdated role template missing "audit.export" permission.
- Added corrected role bindings at org scope only.
- Confirmed that bindings propagate and authz cache invalidation fired (policy evaluator cache flush for org scope).

Verification / validation:
- Customer re-tested "Export audit logs" in Console: success
- Customer re-tested API key rotation endpoint: success
- Confirmed audit log events include authz decision context fields (decision=allow) for both actions.

Access revocation:
- Break-glass role auto-expired.
- Confirmed no lingering elevated session tokens.

Evidence artifacts captured:
- Change record: control-plane audit event IDs (see below)
- Approval trail: recorded in this ticket and BGW request ID
- Post-event review: documented under Comments

Sensitive identifiers (for internal use only):
- BGW request ID: BGW-2025-02-11-1830-PT-0427
- Target org_id: org_7Qp1N (Northbridge Healthcare)
- Target private cluster: nbhc-private-us-west-1
- Audit event IDs:
  - evt_aud_01JMB3H0KZ2R9S6R (binding.read)
  - evt_aud_01JMB3N4F1QX2D8W (binding.create)
  - evt_aud_01JMB3Q9Q0G8C3VJ (audit.export.verify)

Related discussion:
- Slack: #eng-security thread "break-glass procedure and alerting" (ref: sources/slack/eng-security/1739635544-break-glass-procedure-and-alerting.json)
- Slack: #support "customer seeing permission denied after role migration" (ref: sources/slack/support/1740065522-customer-seeing-permission-denied-after-role-migration.json)
